chunks.text: Mounting Home Directory on Login | IRIS Skip to main content Skip to primary navigation Skip to primary sidebar Mounting Home Directory on Login Normally, to do this, you would simply add the folder to your Login Items. However, due to problems with

o your Login Items. However, due to problems with how the accounts are configured, this can not be done so simply. Here is how to work around the problem: Open the “Directory Access” application located in /System/Library/CoreServices/. The following

ed in /System/Library/CoreServices/. The following window will show up. Click the lock button and enter your login/password to unlock it. Once the window is unlocked, select Active Directory and click the little pencil icon to edit the configuration.

the little pencil icon to edit the configuration. Uncheck the Use UNC path from Active Directory to derive network home location. option and press OK. Now connect to your home directory by going to the Finder and choosing Connect to Server… from the

he Finder and choosing Connect to Server… from the menu. This will mount your home directory, which we will now add to your login items. Open “System Preferences…” from the Apple menu and choose Accounts. Choose login items from the tab panel as show

nts. Choose login items from the tab panel as shown below. Press the + button inside the Login Items pane and choose your home directory. Primary Sidebar IRIS Service Status We have 0 Active Incidents, and 1 Scheduled Maintenance noted. IST Service S
, and 1 Scheduled Maintenance noted. IST Service Status Outages to campus services are listed at berkeley.statusdashboard.com .
